1.q: We previously demonstrated that an ERF gene from Tamarix hispida, ThERF1, encodes a protein binding to GCC-box and DRE motifs and negatively modulates abiotic stress tolerance.
we characterized the functions of an ERF gene from Tamarix hispida, ThERF1... ThERF1 can be clustered into class II ERF.

2.q: Microarray analyses showed that many ROS scavenging genes were up-regulated by ThbZIP1 under salt stress conditions... ROS levels in the transgenic plants were significantly reduced.

3.q: Histochemical staining suggested that TheIF1A improves reactive oxygen species (ROS) scavenging in plants.

4.q: Overexpression of ThNAC13 induced superoxide dismutase (SOD) and peroxidase (POD) activities... decreased the reactive oxygen species (ROS) and malondialdehyde levels; and reduced electrolyte leakage rates in both transgenic Tamarix and Arabidopsis plants.

5.q: overexpression of ThNAC4 could improve ROS scavenging enzyme activity to lower ROS accumulation and limit cell damage in transgenic Arabidopsis under salt and osmotic stress conditions.

6.q: ThNAC4 can increase the transcription of the SOD, POD, TPS, and TPP genes, which may contribute to increased SOD and POD activities and trehalose content under salt and drought stress.

7.q: OE transgenic plants could enhance the tolerance to salt stress by improving the scavenging capacity of ROS and reducing the degree of cell damage, while SE transgenic plants showed the opposite.

8.q: These results suggest that ThASR3 decreased ROS accumulation by reducing H2O2 content and enhancing POD and SOD activities in transgenic T. hispida and Arabidopsis.

9.q: These results suggest that overexpression of ThASR3 increases proline and betaine biosynthesis, further contributing to the osmotic potential, eventually improving abiotic stress tolerance.

10.q: ThNAC7 expression results in reduced ROS accumulation in plant cells, mediated by increased POD and SOD activities.

11.q: transgenic plants overexpressing ThHSFA1 displayed enhanced salt tolerance and exhibited reduced reactive oxygen species (ROS) levels and increased antioxidant enzyme activity levels under salt stress.

12.q: Overexpression of the ThCOL2 gene in plants under 150 mM NaCl stress increased the ability of transgenic T. hispida cells to remove reactive oxygen species (ROS) by regulating the activity of protective enzymes and promoting a decrease in the accumulation of O2– and H2O2.

13.q: ThSOS3-overexpressing transgenic T. hispida plants exhibited greater reactive oxygen species (ROS)-scavenging capability and antioxidant enzyme activity, lower MDA and H2O2 levels under salt stress.

14.q: Under salt stress, the staining degree of the OE plants was lighter than that of EV, suggesting that overexpression of ThLBD11 reduced the H2O2 and O2− contents in the OE plants under salt stress.

16.q: Overexpression of ThbHLH1 significantly elevates glycine betaine and proline levels.

17.q: Th2CysPrx in tobacco improved salt stress tolerance by increasing ROS scavenging and preventing cell damage

19.q: ThSOS1, ThSOS2, and ThSOS3 genes were closely related to the SOS1, SOS2, and SOS3 subfamilies in Arabidopsis. ThSOS3 belonged to the CBL4 subfamily.

20.q: The RT-qPCR and ChIP-PCR results indicated that ThLBD11 negatively regulated the expression of ThAHL27 and ThATPD by binding directly to their promoter fragments containing the CGGC motif.

21.q: The RT-qPCR and ChIP-PCR results indicated that ThLBD11 negatively regulated the expression of ThAHL27 and ThATPD by binding directly to their promoter fragments containing the CGGC motif.

22.q: RNA-seq analysis of transient transgenic T. hispida overexpressing ThSCL32 revealed significantly enhanced ThPHD3 (prolyl-4-hydroxylase domain 3 protein) gene expression.

23.q: the expressions of ThNCED1 and ThNCED4 were significantly higher... in OE compared with CON under normal conditions.

24.q: the expressions of ThNCED1 and ThNCED4 were significantly higher... in OE compared with CON under normal conditions.

25.q: transient overexpression of ThDof1.4 increased the transcripts of ThZFP1; conversely, transient RNAi-silencing of ThDof1.4 reduced the expression of ThZFP1.

26.q: The expression levels of these genes were 1.22, 1.55, 1.50, and 1.86-fold that of CK plants, respectively. These results indicated that Th2CysPrx overexpression affected ThGSTZ1, ThGPX, ThSOD and ThPOD gene expression.

27.q: The expression levels of these genes were 1.22, 1.55, 1.50, and 1.86-fold that of CK plants, respectively. These results indicated that Th2CysPrx overexpression affected ThGSTZ1, ThGPX, ThSOD and ThPOD gene expression.

29.q: The expression levels of these genes were 1.22, 1.55, 1.50, and 1.86-fold that of CK plants, respectively. These results indicated that Th2CysPrx overexpression affected ThGSTZ1, ThGPX, ThSOD and ThPOD gene expression.

30.q: ThABF1 could bind to the truncated promoter of ThbZIP1 containing ABRE motifs but not to the promoter lacking ABRE motifs, as shown by Y1H analysis and ChIP.

31.q: These data clearly indicated that ThMYC6 can activate gene expression through its interaction with the ThbZIP1 promoter via binding to the E-box motifs.

32.q: Two MYC genes (ThMYC4 and ThMYC6) were found to bind to the E-box motif... in the promoter of ThbZIP1.

33.q: ThDof could bind to a truncated TheIF1A promoter retaining the Dof motif but could not bind to promoter fragments lacking the motif or with a mutated Dof motif, suggesting that ThDof may activate expression of TheIF1A by binding to this motif.

34.q: ThDOF8 binds to the TGCG element in the promoter regions of its target genes... ThDOF8 binds to the TGCG cis-element in the promoter of the target gene CRK10 to regulate its expression

35.q: Expression profiling by gene chip analysis identified four potential direct targets of ThDOF8, namely CRK10 and CRK26, and two glutamate decarboxylase genes, GAD41, and GAD42, further verified by ChIP–quantitative-PCR, EMSAs, Y1H assays, and β-glucuronidase enzyme activity assays.

36.q: Expression profiling by gene chip analysis identified four potential direct targets of ThDOF8, namely CRK10 and CRK26, and two glutamate decarboxylase genes, GAD41, and GAD42, further verified by ChIP–quantitative-PCR, EMSAs, Y1H assays, and β-glucuronidase enzyme activity assays.

38.q: ChIP-PCR further verified that ThSCL32 probably binds to the novel cis-element SBS (ACGTTG) in the promoter of ThPHD3 to activate its expression.

39.q: ThHSFA1 protein acts as an upstream regulator that can directly activate ThWRKY4 expression by binding to the heat shock element (HSE) of the ThWRKY4 promoter using yeast one-hybrid (Y1H), chromatin immunoprecipitation (ChIP), and dual-luciferase reporter assays.

40.q: Chromatin immunoprecipitation indicated that ThDof1.4 binds to the ThZFP1 promoter... ThDof1.4 could activate the expression of ThZFP1 by binding to the Dof elements present in the ThZFP1 promoter.

41.q: two downstream target genes (ThAHL27 and ThATPD) of ThLBD11 in the gene regulatory network were confirmed... by binding directly to their promoter fragments containing the CGGC motif.

42.q: ChIP-PCR results indicated that ThLBD11 negatively regulated the expression of ThAHL27 and ThATPD by binding directly to their promoter fragments containing the CGGC motif.

43.q: Overexpression of ThbHLH1 significantly elevates glycine betaine and proline levels, increases Ca2+ concentration and enhances peroxidase (POD) and superoxide dismutase (SOD) activities to decrease reactive oxygen species (ROS) accumulation.

46.q: ThERF1 negatively regulates abiotic stress tolerance by strongly inhibiting the expression of SOD and POD genes, leading to decreased ROS-scavenging ability.
